def upload_file_to_gcs(
    storage_client: Any,
    *,
    bucket_name: str,
    destination_blob: str,
    local_path: Path,
) -> str:
    bucket = storage_client.bucket(bucket_name)
    blob = bucket.blob(destination_blob)
    blob.upload_from_filename(str(local_path))
    return f"gs://{bucket_name}/{destination_blob}"


def process_drive_file(
    *,
    project_id: str,
    bucket: str,
    gcs_prefix: str,
    file_metadata: dict[str, Any],
    temp_dir_path: Path,
    ingested_at: str,
    include_raw_payload: bool,
    assume_canonical_terms: bool,
    analysis_config: OrthogonalityIngestConfig | None,
    service_account_file: str | None,
    service_account_subject: str | None,
    log_level: str,
) -> ProcessedDriveFileResult:
    configure_worker_logging(log_level)
    libs = _import_google_libs()
    google_auth_default_fn = libs["google_auth_default"]
    service_account_credentials_cls = libs["service_account_credentials"]
    drive_builder = libs["build"]
    storage_module = libs["storage"]
    media_downloader_class = libs["MediaIoBaseDownload"]

    credentials = build_credentials_from_values(
        service_account_file=service_account_file,
        service_account_subject=service_account_subject,
        google_auth_default_fn=google_auth_default_fn,
        service_account_credentials_cls=service_account_credentials_cls,
    )

    file_id = str(file_metadata.get("id", ""))
    file_name = str(file_metadata.get("name", ""))
    md5_checksum = str(file_metadata.get("md5Checksum", ""))
    modified_time = str(file_metadata.get("modifiedTime", ""))
    size_raw = file_metadata.get("size")
    file_size_bytes: int | None = None
    if size_raw is not None and str(size_raw).strip():
        file_size_bytes = int(size_raw)

    if not file_id or not file_name:
        raise IngestError(f"Malformed Drive file metadata: {file_metadata}")

    local_input = temp_dir_path / f"{file_id}.csv"
    local_output = temp_dir_path / f"{file_id}.transformed.csv"
    local_analysis_output = temp_dir_path / f"{file_id}.orthogonality.ndjson"

    drive_service = drive_builder("drive", "v3", credentials=credentials, cache_discovery=False)
    storage_client = storage_module.Client(project=project_id, credentials=credentials)

    LOGGER.info("Downloading Drive file %s (%s)", file_name, file_id)
    download_started_at = time.perf_counter()
    downloaded_bytes = download_drive_file(
        drive_service,
        media_downloader_class,
        file_id=file_id,
        file_name=file_name,
        destination=local_input,
        total_size_bytes=file_size_bytes,
    )
    download_elapsed_seconds = max(0.001, time.perf_counter() - download_started_at)
    LOGGER.info(
        "Download complete %s (%s): size=%s elapsed=%ss rate=%s/s",
        file_name,
        file_id,
        _format_bytes(downloaded_bytes),
        round(download_elapsed_seconds, 2),
        _format_bytes(downloaded_bytes / download_elapsed_seconds),
    )

    LOGGER.info("Transforming file %s (%s)", file_name, file_id)
    transform_started_at = time.perf_counter()
    row_count, analysis_summary = transform_csv_to_base_format(
        local_input,
        local_output,
        source_file_id=file_id,
        source_file_name=file_name,
        ingested_at=ingested_at,
        include_raw_payload=include_raw_payload,
        assume_canonical_terms=assume_canonical_terms,
        analysis_output_path=local_analysis_output if analysis_config is not None else None,
        analysis_config=analysis_config,
    )
    transform_elapsed_seconds = max(0.001, time.perf_counter() - transform_started_at)
    transformed_size_bytes = local_output.stat().st_size
    LOGGER.info(
        "Transform complete %s (%s): rows=%d output_size=%s elapsed=%ss",
        file_name,
        file_id,
        row_count,
        _format_bytes(transformed_size_bytes),
        round(transform_elapsed_seconds, 2),
    )
    if analysis_config is not None:
        LOGGER.info(
            "Analysis summary %s (%s): rows_succeeded=%d rows_failed=%d error_counts=%s",
            file_name,
            file_id,
            analysis_summary.rows_succeeded,
            analysis_summary.rows_failed,
            json.dumps(analysis_summary.error_counts, sort_keys=True),
        )

    destination_blob = f"{gcs_prefix.rstrip('/')}/{file_id}_{_safe_object_name(file_name)}"
    LOGGER.info(
        "Uploading transformed file %s (%s): destination=gs://%s/%s size=%s",
        file_name,
        file_id,
        bucket,
        destination_blob,
        _format_bytes(transformed_size_bytes),
    )
    upload_started_at = time.perf_counter()
    gcs_uri = upload_file_to_gcs(
        storage_client,
        bucket_name=bucket,
        destination_blob=destination_blob,
        local_path=local_output,
    )
    upload_elapsed_seconds = max(0.001, time.perf_counter() - upload_started_at)
    LOGGER.info(
        "Upload complete %s (%s): elapsed=%ss rate=%s/s",
        file_name,
        file_id,
        round(upload_elapsed_seconds, 2),
        _format_bytes(transformed_size_bytes / upload_elapsed_seconds),
    )
    analysis_gcs_uri: str | None = None
    if analysis_config is not None and analysis_summary.rows_succeeded > 0 and local_analysis_output.exists():
        analysis_destination_blob = (
            f"{gcs_prefix.rstrip('/')}/analysis_orthogonality/{file_id}_{_safe_object_name(file_name)}.ndjson"
        )
        LOGGER.info(
            "Uploading orthogonality sidecar %s (%s): destination=gs://%s/%s size=%s",
            file_name,
            file_id,
            bucket,
            analysis_destination_blob,
            _format_bytes(local_analysis_output.stat().st_size),
        )
        try:
            analysis_gcs_uri = upload_file_to_gcs(
                storage_client,
                bucket_name=bucket,
                destination_blob=analysis_destination_blob,
                local_path=local_analysis_output,
            )
        except Exception as exc:
            analysis_summary.drain_successes_into_failures(
                error_code="analysis_upload_failure",
                error_message=str(exc),
                sample_limit=analysis_config.debug_sample_limit,
            )
            if analysis_config.mode == "required":
                raise
            LOGGER.warning("Orthogonality sidecar upload failed for %s (%s): %s", file_name, file_id, exc)

    cleanup_paths = [local_input, local_output]
    if local_analysis_output.exists():
        cleanup_paths.append(local_analysis_output)
    reclaimed_bytes = cleanup_local_files(cleanup_paths)
    if reclaimed_bytes > 0:
        LOGGER.info(
            "Local cleanup complete %s (%s): reclaimed=%s",
            file_name,
            file_id,
            _format_bytes(reclaimed_bytes),
        )

    manifest_row = {
        "file_id": file_id,
        "file_name": file_name,
        "md5_checksum": md5_checksum,
        "modified_time": modified_time,
        "gcs_uri": gcs_uri,
        "ingested_at": ingested_at,
    }
    return ProcessedDriveFileResult(
        gcs_uri=gcs_uri,
        manifest_row=manifest_row,
        row_count=row_count,
        downloaded_bytes=downloaded_bytes,
        analysis_gcs_uri=analysis_gcs_uri,
        analysis_summary=analysis_summary,
    )

class OrthogonalitySidecarWriter:
    def __init__(self, path: Path, *, config: OrthogonalityIngestConfig) -> None:
        self.path = path
        self.path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self._file = self.path.open("w", encoding="utf-8")
        self._config = config
        self._summary = AnalysisSummary()
        self._pending_rows: list[tuple[dict[str, Any], str, tuple[Any, Any, Any, Any, Any]]] = []

    @property
    def summary(self) -> AnalysisSummary:
        return self._summary

    def analyze_record(self, base_record: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
        try:
            decision = classify_for_stream(
                l=base_record["l"],
                m=base_record["m"],
                k=base_record["k"],
                n=base_record["n"],
                a_terms=base_record["a_terms"],
                b_terms=base_record["b_terms"],
            )
        except Exception as exc:
            self._summary.record_failure(
                error_code="analysis_row_error",
                record=base_record,
                error_message=str(exc),
                sample_limit=self._config.debug_sample_limit,
            )
            return

        if decision.rust_call is None:
            self._write_result(
                base_record,
                orthogonal=bool(decision.orthogonal),
                odd_weight=None,
                decision_source=decision.source,
            )
            return

        self._pending_rows.append((base_record, decision.source, decision.rust_call))
        if len(self._pending_rows) >= self._config.rust_batch_size:
            self.flush()

    def flush(self) -> None:
        if not self._pending_rows:
            return
        pending_rows = self._pending_rows
        self._pending_rows = []
        calls = [rust_call for _record, _source, rust_call in pending_rows]
        try:
            replies = get_rust_eval_client(self._config.rust_eval_library).evaluate_analysis_batch(calls)
        except Exception as exc:
            for base_record, _source, _rust_call in pending_rows:
                self._summary.record_failure(
                    error_code="rust_eval_failure",
                    record=base_record,
                    error_message=str(exc),
                    sample_limit=self._config.debug_sample_limit,
                )
            return

        for (base_record, decision_source, _rust_call), (orthogonal, odd_weight) in zip(pending_rows, replies):
            self._write_result(
                base_record,
                orthogonal=orthogonal,
                odd_weight=odd_weight,
                decision_source=decision_source,
            )

    def _write_result(
        self,
        base_record: dict[str, Any],
        *,
        orthogonal: bool,
        odd_weight: bool | None,
        decision_source: str,
    ) -> None:
        result_record = _orthogonality_result_record(
            base_record,
            config=self._config,
            orthogonal=orthogonal,
            odd_weight=odd_weight,
            decision_source=decision_source,
        )
        self._file.write(json.dumps(result_record, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=True, sort_keys=True))
        self._file.write("\n")
        self._summary.record_success()

    def close(self) -> AnalysisSummary:
        self.flush()
        self._file.close()
        return self._summary


def transform_csv_to_base_format(
    input_csv_path: Path,
    output_csv_path: Path,
    *,
    source_file_id: str,
    source_file_name: str,
    ingested_at: str,
    include_raw_payload: bool,
    assume_canonical_terms: bool,
    analysis_output_path: Path | None = None,
    analysis_config: OrthogonalityIngestConfig | None = None,
) -> tuple[int, AnalysisSummary]:
    row_count = 0
    started_at = time.perf_counter()
    last_log_time = started_at
    last_log_row_count = 0
    input_size_bytes = max(1, input_csv_path.stat().st_size)
    analysis_summary = AnalysisSummary()
    analysis_writer: OrthogonalitySidecarWriter | None = None
    if analysis_config is not None:
        if analysis_output_path is None:
            raise IngestError("analysis_output_path is required when analysis_config is set")
        analysis_writer = OrthogonalitySidecarWriter(analysis_output_path, config=analysis_config)

    with input_csv_path.open("r", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as input_file:
        reader = csv.DictReader(input_file)
        if not reader.fieldnames:
            raise IngestError(f"CSV file has no header row: {input_csv_path}")
        field_name_mapping, renamed_pairs, by_canonical = build_field_name_mapping(reader.fieldnames)
        if renamed_pairs:
            LOGGER.info(
                "Header normalization %s (%s): %s",
                source_file_name,
                source_file_id,
                ", ".join(f"{src}->{dst}" for src, dst in sorted(set(renamed_pairs))),
            )
        duplicated_targets = {
            canonical: sources for canonical, sources in by_canonical.items() if canonical and len(sources) > 1
        }
        if duplicated_targets:
            LOGGER.warning(
                "Header collisions after normalization for %s (%s): %s",
                source_file_name,
                source_file_id,
                duplicated_targets,
            )

        with output_csv_path.open("w", newline="", encoding="utf-8") as output_file:
            writer = csv.DictWriter(output_file, fieldnames=BASE_COLUMNS)
            writer.writeheader()

            for row_number, row in enumerate(reader, start=1):
                normalized_row = normalize_row_field_names(row, field_name_mapping)
                base_record = _row_to_base_record(
                    normalized_row,
                    source_file_id=source_file_id,
                    source_file_name=source_file_name,
                    source_row_number=row_number,
                    ingested_at=ingested_at,
                    include_raw_payload=include_raw_payload,
                    assume_canonical_terms=assume_canonical_terms,
                )
                writer.writerow(base_record)
                row_count += 1
                if analysis_writer is not None:
                    analysis_writer.analyze_record(base_record)

                now = time.perf_counter()
                should_log = False
                if row_count - last_log_row_count >= TRANSFORM_PROGRESS_LOG_ROWS:
                    should_log = True
                if now - last_log_time >= TRANSFORM_PROGRESS_LOG_SECONDS:
                    should_log = True

                if should_log:
                    elapsed = max(0.001, now - started_at)
                    rows_per_second = row_count / elapsed
                    percent_complete: float | None = None
                    try:
                        bytes_consumed = input_file.buffer.tell()
                        percent_complete = min(100.0, max(0.0, (bytes_consumed / input_size_bytes) * 100.0))
                    except Exception:
                        percent_complete = None

                    if percent_complete is None:
                        LOGGER.info(
                            "Transform progress %s (%s): rows=%d rate=%.1f rows/s",
                            source_file_name,
                            source_file_id,
                            row_count,
                            rows_per_second,
                        )
                    else:
                        LOGGER.info(
                            "Transform progress %s (%s): %.1f%% rows=%d rate=%.1f rows/s",
                            source_file_name,
                            source_file_id,
                            percent_complete,
                            row_count,
                            rows_per_second,
                        )
                    last_log_time = now
                    last_log_row_count = row_count

    if analysis_writer is not None:
        analysis_summary = analysis_writer.close()
    return row_count, analysis_summary


def _safe_object_name(file_name: str) -> str:
    return "".join(ch if ch.isalnum() or ch in {"-", "_", "."} else "_" for ch in file_name)

payload.input:
*** Begin Patch
*** Add File: /home/wecker/git/qec_team/qeclib/dbwz8/csvdb/INGEST_QDIST_INTEGRATION_PLAN.md
+# Ingest-Time QDist Integration Plan
+
+## Objective
+
+When new codes are ingested into `codes_base`, run the same distance-analysis workflow during that ingest run:
+
+- produce QDist attempts (`code_distance_attempts`)
+- update conservative best-known summaries (`code_distance_summary`)
+- keep run-level provenance and failure accounting aligned with existing ingest-time analysis patterns
+
+This makes new rows analysis-ready immediately after ingest finalization.
+
+## Scope
+
+In scope:
+
+- Integrate QDist into `src/csvdb/ingest.py` using the existing ingest analysis model (`off|best_effort|required`).
+- Reuse typed distance data model and merge logic from `src/csvdb/distance_pipeline.py`.
+- Process only rows touched by the current ingest run.
+
+Out of scope:
+
+- Replacing the large cluster refresh path.
+- Recomputing global frontier tables for all historical rows on every ingest run.
+
+## Design Principles
+
+1. Reuse existing contracts:
+   - `DistanceAttemptRecord`
+   - `DistanceSummaryRecord`
+   - `build_conservative_summary_rows`
+   - staging/merge helpers for attempts and summary
+2. Keep ingest idempotent:
+   - deterministic run IDs
+   - merge-based upserts
+   - no destructive rewrites of historical attempts
+3. Keep ingest operationally safe:
+   - bounded compute per row
+   - `best_effort` mode to avoid blocking base ingest
+   - `required` mode for strict environments
+
+## Proposed End-to-End Flow
+
+### 1) Add distance analysis mode to ingest
+
+Mirror the orthogonality pattern:
+
+- `CSVDB_INGEST_DISTANCE_MODE` with choices: `off`, `best_effort`, `required`
+- `DistanceIngestConfig` dataclass (algorithm version, params, worker count, budgets)
+- `analysis_name = "distance_qdist"` row in `analysis_runs`
+
+### 2) Define current-ingest row scope
+
+After loading transformed files into `_staging_codes_base` and merging into `codes_base`:
+
+- materialize a temporary scope table for this ingest run (row IDs + core fields)
+- scope source should be rows from this run (from staging rows and/or `ingested_at == ingest_time`)
+- this scope becomes the only input for ingest-time distance work
+
+### 3) Build ingest-scoped distance workset
+
+Use a new helper in `distance_pipeline` that matches existing full-refresh logic but restricts to ingest scope:
+
+- `acceptance_distance = COALESCE(code_distance_summary.best_distance_estimate, codes_base.min_distance_guess)`
+- `search_target_distance = GREATEST(acceptance_distance - search_target_slack, 0)`
+- include all structured QDist input fields and selection metadata (`selection_rule = "ingest_scope"`)
+
+Rows with no usable acceptance estimate are skipped and counted in metrics.
+
+### 4) Execute QDist during ingest finalization
+
+Run QDist worker shards locally as part of ingest finalization (not a separate manual pipeline):
+
+- shard ingest-scoped workset evenly
+- run `csvdb-qdist-worker` over shards with bounded runtime / iteration policy
+- emit per-shard artifacts:
+  - `attempts.ndjson`
+  - `summary.ndjson` (from conservative summary builder)
+  - `worker_metrics.json`
+
+Failure handling:
+
+- `best_effort`: continue ingest, record failed rows and error codes
+- `required`: fail ingest if distance execution/persist fails
+
+### 5) Persist distance outputs in the same ingest run
+
+During finalization, add distance phases after base merge:
+
+1. Load attempts NDJSON -> `_staging_code_distance_attempts_*`
+2. Merge into `code_distance_attempts`
+3. Load summary NDJSON -> `_staging_code_distance_summary_*`
+4. Merge into `code_distance_summary`
+
+Reuse existing `distance_pipeline` load/merge helpers; avoid duplicating SQL.
+
+### 6) Frontier and prioritization updates (incremental)
+
+Do not rebuild global frontier every ingest.
+
+Instead:
+
+- track affected `n` values from current ingest scope
+- optionally refresh only ingest-scoped frontier candidate tables
+- keep full global frontier rebuild as a separate scheduled job
+
+## Implementation Plan
+
+## Phase 1: Configuration + run wiring
+
+- Add distance mode/env options to `parse_args()`.
+- Add `DistanceIngestConfig` and run ID builder.
+- Extend ingest metrics and `analysis_runs` emission for distance.
+
+Exit criteria:
+
+- In `off` mode, behavior is unchanged.
+- New config appears in ingest summary logs.
+
+## Phase 2: Ingest-scoped workset creation
+
+- Add helper to materialize ingest-scoped distance workset table.
+- Include deterministic selection metadata and policy fields.
+- Add row-count and skipped-row metrics.
+
+Exit criteria:
+
+- Workset row count matches expected ingest-scoped eligible rows.
+
+## Phase 3: QDist execution + sidecar outputs
+
+- Add finalization runner that shards workset and executes QDist worker.
+- Generate attempts and conservative summary NDJSON artifacts.
+- Aggregate worker metrics.
+
+Exit criteria:
+
+- Shard outputs are valid and parseable by typed record validators.
+
+## Phase 4: Load/merge persistence
+
+- Load attempts/summary artifacts into staging.
+- Merge into canonical distance tables.
+- Record inserted/updated counts in ingest metrics.
+
+Exit criteria:
+
+- New ingest rows have corresponding distance attempts and summary rows.
+
+## Phase 5: Reliability and guardrails
+
+- Add retry policy for transient worker/load failures.
+- Enforce budgets (max rows, max runtime, max retries).
+- Ensure `best_effort` vs `required` semantics match orthogonality behavior.
+
+Exit criteria:
+
+- Failure modes are deterministic and observable in logs + `analysis_runs`.
+
+## Testing Plan
+
+Unit tests:
+
+- config parsing and mode semantics
+- ingest-scoped workset SQL restriction logic
+- conservative summary behavior on mixed success/failure attempts
+- finalization metric aggregation
+
+Integration tests:
+
+- smoke ingest (`--max-files 1`) with distance mode `best_effort`
+- verify merged rows in:
+  - `code_distance_attempts`
+  - `code_distance_summary`
+
+Operational checks:
+
+- compare ingest elapsed time with/without distance mode
+- verify no duplicate-attempt explosion on reruns
+
+## Rollout Plan
+
+1. Ship behind `CSVDB_INGEST_DISTANCE_MODE=off` default.
+2. Enable `best_effort` on smoke ingest runs.
+3. Enable `best_effort` on larger ingest with bounded budgets.
+4. Promote to `required` once stability and runtime are acceptable.
+
+## Open Decisions
+
+1. Default QDist parameter budget for ingest mode (iterations, hit caps, timeout).
+2. Whether to run only one attempt per new row during ingest or multi-attempt policy.
+3. Whether ingest should block until all distance work is persisted, or allow deferred completion in extreme backlog cases.
*** End Patch

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

The `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. - Begin with a concise checklist (3-7 bullets) of what you will do; keep items conceptual, not implementation-level.
- Keep intermediary progress updates to one short line at major milestones only.
- Always run `git status` before using `git add -A` or `git add .` to verify which files will be staged. This prevents accidentally adding unwanted files to your commit.
- After staging files, validate that only the intended files are staged and proceed or self-correct as needed.
- Never amend a commit once it is made. Commit history should remain unchanged to maintain the integrity of the project history.
- Do not run `python`, `python3`, `pip`, or `pip3` directly. Always use `uv run` or `uv sync --all-extras` to ensure the correct virtual environment and dependencies are used for the project.
- Most projects utilize `pre-commit` hooks to automate code formatting and linting.
- Only make the minimal necessary changes to complete your task.
- Avoid making changes that are not directly related to the task at hand.

--- project-doc ---

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Development Rules

- Keep it DRY - reuse code, check for existing patterns before adding new ones
- Implement the simplest solution; don't generalize until needed
- Only implement what's asked for, nothing extra
- Always run `pytest` before claiming a task is done
- Run `pre-commit run --all-files` before committing
- Use `git add <specific-file>` not `git add .`

## Build & Development Commands

```bash
# Install with uv (recommended - Python 3.12 has all wheels pre-built)
uv sync -p 3.12 --all-extras

# Install with micromamba (recommended for full compatibility)
unidep install -n qeclib -e .
micromamba activate qeclib

# Run all tests with parallel execution
uv run pytest -n auto

# Run a single test file
uv run pytest tests/test_stabilizer_code.py

# Run a specific test
uv run pytest tests/test_stabilizer_code.py::test_function_name -v

# Run tests with more repetitions (for randomized tests)
uv run pytest --n-repeats-low=10 --n-repeats-high=100

# Lint and format
ruff check --fix .
ruff format .

# Type checking
pyright
```

## Architecture Overview

**qeclib** is a quantum error correction (QEC) library for simulating and analyzing stabilizer codes.

### Core Class Hierarchy

```
StabilizerCode (base class for all stabilizer codes)
├── CssCode (CSS codes with separate X/Z generators)
│   └── HypergraphProductCode (product construction)
└── SubsystemCode
    └── CssSubsystemCode
```

### Key Modules

- **`qeclib/codes/`**: QEC code implementations
  - `factory.py`: `StabilizerCodeFactory` - primary entry point for constructing codes (surface codes, BB codes, hypergraph products, etc.)
  - `stabilizer_code.py`: Base `StabilizerCode` class with stabilizer generators, logical operators, minimum distance computation
  - `css_code.py`: `CssCode` for codes with pure X and Z generators
  - `BB_code.py`: Bivariate Bicycle codes from IBM's work

- **`qeclib/utils/`**: Mathematical primitives
  - `binary_matrix.py`: `BinaryMatrix` for GF(2) linear algebra
  - `pauli_matrix.py`: `PauliMatrix` for Pauli operator computations
  - `pauli_string.py`: `PauliString` representation (e.g., "XZIY")

- **`qeclib/circuits/`**: Circuit construction
  - `syndrome_measurement_circuit_builders/`: Strategies for syndrome extraction (edge coloring, sequential)
  - `noisy_circuit_builders/`: Noise model injection

- **`qeclib/noise/`**: Noise models
  - `circuit_models.py`: Ion chain noise models with operation constraints
  - `executable_code.py`: Executable QEC code with noise injection

- **`qeclib/decoders/`**: Error correction decoders
  - `beamsearch/`: C++ beam search decoder with Python bindings (compiled via custom Hatch build hook)

- **`qeclib/simulations/`**: Simulation runners for memory experiments and code capacity analysis

- **`qeclib/resource_estimation/`**: Resource analysis and time estimation for QEC architectures

### External Dependencies

- **stim**: Core quantum circuit simulator
- **sinter**: Decoder interface for large-scale simulations
- **pymatching**: Minimum-weight perfect matching decoder
- **cirq-core**: Quantum circuit representation

### Code Patterns

1. **Factory pattern**: Use `StabilizerCodeFactory.build_*()` methods to construct codes
2. **Cached properties**: Expensive computations (minimum distance, decoders) are lazily evaluated and cached
3. **Validation in constructors**: Stabilizer codes validate commutation and length constraints on initialization

### Test Infrastructure

- Tests use `pytest-randomly` for random seed control - access via the `seed` fixture
- Use `seed_repeat` fixture for tests that need different seeds across repetitions
- `square_hgp` fixture creates hypergraph product codes from classical LDPC matrices in `tests/data/classical_ldpc_codes/`


## Skills
A skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.
### Available skills
- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/wecker/git/dotfiles/configs/codex/codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)
- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/wecker/git/dotfiles/configs/codex/codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)
### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.

- Review current ingest architecture and existing SLURM sharding patterns in this repo.

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

usage() {
  cat <<'USAGE'
Usage:
  scripts/launch_analysis_shards.sh [options]

Options:
  --workers N          Number of local worker tabs to launch (default: 8)
  --export-shards N    Number of hash-sharded export queries (default: worker count)
  --run-id ID          Run identifier (default: UTC timestamp)
  --session NAME       zellij session name (default: CSVDB_ANALYSIS_SESSION or bb-analysis)
  --gcloud-config DIR  Optional CLOUDSDK_CONFIG to export in workers
  --notes TEXT         Optional run note stored with the run record
  --row-limit N        Optional export LIMIT for a smoke run
  --keep-downloads     Keep downloaded parquet files on local disk
  --skip-export        Skip the export/init phase and launch workers only
  --dry-run            Print commands without launching zellij
  -h, --help           Show this help

Behavior:
  1) Creates/resets the run record.
  2) Exports the projected orthogonality snapshot to GCS and builds chunk metadata.
  3) Opens a zellij session with one worker tab per local worker.
  4) Opens a lightweight status tab that polls chunk completion.
  5) The launcher attaches to the session; disconnecting later leaves the run alive.
USAGE
}

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
source "$SCRIPT_DIR/csvdb_env_defaults.sh"

WORKERS="${CSVDB_ANALYSIS_WORKERS:-8}"
EXPORT_SHARDS=""
RUN_ID="$(date -u +%Y%m%dT%H%M%SZ)"
SESSION="${CSVDB_ANALYSIS_SESSION:-bb-analysis}"
GCLOUD_CONFIG="${CLOUDSDK_CONFIG:-}"
NOTES=""
ROW_LIMIT=""
KEEP_DOWNLOADS=0
SKIP_EXPORT=0
DRY_RUN=0

is_nonneg_int() {
  local value="$1"
  [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]
}

zellij_session_exists() {
  zellij list-sessions --short 2>/dev/null | grep -Fx -- "$1" >/dev/null
}

kdl_escape() {
  local value="$1"
  value="${value//\\/\\\\}"
  value="${value//\"/\\\"}"
  value="${value//$'\n'/ }"
  printf '%s' "$value"
}

write_zellij_layout() {
  local layout_file="$1"

  {
    printf 'layout {\n'
    printf '    default_tab_template {\n'
    printf '        pane size=1 borderless=true {\n'
    printf '            plugin location="tab-bar"\n'
    printf '        }\n'
    printf '        children\n'
    printf '        pane size=1 borderless=true {\n'
    printf '            plugin location="status-bar"\n'
    printf '        }\n'
    printf '    }\n'
    for ((idx=0; idx<WORKERS; idx++)); do
      printf '    tab name="worker-%d" {\n' "$idx"
      printf '        pane command="bash" close_on_exit=false {\n'
      printf '            args "-lc" "%s"\n' "$(kdl_escape "$(build_worker_cmd "$idx")")"
      printf '        }\n'
      printf '    }\n'
    done
    printf '    tab name="status" {\n'
    printf '        pane command="bash" close_on_exit=false {\n'
    printf '            args "-lc" "%s"\n' "$(kdl_escape "$status_cmd")"
    printf '        }\n'
    printf '    }\n'
    printf '}\n'
  } >"$layout_file"
}

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --workers)
      WORKERS="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --run-id)
      RUN_ID="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --export-shards)
      EXPORT_SHARDS="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --session)
      SESSION="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --gcloud-config)
      GCLOUD_CONFIG="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --notes)
      NOTES="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --row-limit)
      ROW_LIMIT="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --keep-downloads)
      KEEP_DOWNLOADS=1
      shift
      ;;
    --skip-export)
      SKIP_EXPORT=1
      shift
      ;;
    --dry-run)
      DRY_RUN=1
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 2
      ;;
  esac
done

if ! is_nonneg_int "$WORKERS" || [[ "$WORKERS" -lt 1 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: --workers must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [[ -z "$EXPORT_SHARDS" ]]; then
  EXPORT_SHARDS="$WORKERS"
fi
if ! is_nonneg_int "$EXPORT_SHARDS" || [[ "$EXPORT_SHARDS" -lt 1 ]]; then
  echo "ERROR: --export-shards must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi
if [[ -n "$ROW_LIMIT" ]] && { ! is_nonneg_int "$ROW_LIMIT" || [[ "$ROW_LIMIT" -lt 1 ]]; }; then
  echo "ERROR: --row-limit must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi

if ! command -v zellij >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "ERROR: zellij is required." >&2
  exit 1
fi

if zellij_session_exists "$SESSION"; then
  echo "ERROR: zellij session '$SESSION' already exists. Choose --session NAME or kill it first." >&2
  exit 1
fi

base_cmd_prefix="cd $(printf '%q' "$PWD")"
if [[ -n "$GCLOUD_CONFIG" ]]; then
  base_cmd_prefix+="; export CLOUDSDK_CONFIG=$(printf '%q' "$GCLOUD_CONFIG")"
fi
base_cmd_prefix+="; source scripts/csvdb_env_defaults.sh"

init_cmd="${base_cmd_prefix}; scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh init --run-id $(printf '%q' "$RUN_ID") --worker-count ${WORKERS}"
if [[ -n "$NOTES" ]]; then
  init_cmd+=" --notes $(printf '%q' "$NOTES")"
fi

export_cmd="${base_cmd_prefix}; scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh export --run-id $(printf '%q' "$RUN_ID")"
export_cmd+=" --export-shards ${EXPORT_SHARDS}"
if [[ -n "$ROW_LIMIT" ]]; then
  export_cmd+=" --row-limit ${ROW_LIMIT}"
fi

build_worker_cmd() {
  local idx="$1"
  local cmd="${base_cmd_prefix}; scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh worker --run-id $(printf '%q' "$RUN_ID") --worker-index ${idx} --worker-count ${WORKERS}"
  if [[ "$KEEP_DOWNLOADS" -eq 1 ]]; then
    cmd+=" --keep-downloads"
  fi
  cmd+=" 2>&1 | tee /tmp/bb_analysis_worker_${idx}.log"
  printf '%s' "$cmd"
}

status_cmd="${base_cmd_prefix}; while true; do clear; scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh status --run-id $(printf '%q' "$RUN_ID"); sleep 30; done"

echo "Launching ${WORKERS} workers in zellij session '${SESSION}' for run '${RUN_ID}'"

if [[ "$DRY_RUN" -eq 1 ]]; then
  echo "[init] $init_cmd"
  if [[ "$SKIP_EXPORT" -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "[export] $export_cmd"
  fi
  for ((idx=0; idx<WORKERS; idx++)); do
    echo "[worker-${idx}] $(build_worker_cmd "$idx")"
  done
  echo "[status] $status_cmd"
  echo "[zellij] zellij -s $SESSION -n <generated-layout>"
  exit 0
fi

eval "$init_cmd"
if [[ "$SKIP_EXPORT" -eq 0 ]]; then
  eval "$export_cmd"
fi

layout_file="$(mktemp "${TMPDIR:-/tmp}/csvdb_analysis_zellij.XXXXXX.kdl")"
cleanup() {
  rm -f "$layout_file"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

write_zellij_layout "$layout_file"
zellij setup --dump-layout "$layout_file" >/dev/null

echo "Opening zellij session '$SESSION'..."
echo "Reattach later with: zellij attach $SESSION"
zellij -s "$SESSION" -n "$layout_file"

echo "zellij session: $SESSION"
echo "run_id: $RUN_ID"
echo "status: scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh status --run-id $RUN_ID"

#!/usr/bin/env bash
set -euo pipefail

usage() {
  cat <<'EOF'
Usage:
  scripts/monitor_analysis_progress.sh [options]

Options:
  --shard-count N          Shard count for the active backfill run. Required.
  --interval-seconds N     Delay between snapshots (default: 1800).
  --rate-window-seconds N  Rate sampling window for each snapshot (default: 60).
  --log-file PATH          Destination log file (default: /tmp/bb_analysis_monitor.log).
  --iterations N           Number of snapshots to record (default: 0 = run forever).
  --progress-mode MODE     Snapshot mode: light | global (default: light).
  --skip-initial           Wait one full interval before the first snapshot.
  -h, --help               Show this help message.

Each snapshot appends:
  1. A local shard-state summary
  2. `scripts/check_analysis_rate.sh`
  3. Optionally, `scripts/run_analysis_backfill.sh --phase progress`

Examples:
  scripts/monitor_analysis_progress.sh --shard-count 6
  scripts/monitor_analysis_progress.sh --shard-count 6 --iterations 4
EOF
}

INTERVAL_SECONDS=1800
RATE_WINDOW_SECONDS=60
LOG_FILE="/tmp/bb_analysis_monitor.log"
ITERATIONS=0
SHARD_COUNT=""
SKIP_INITIAL=0
PROGRESS_MODE="light"

is_pos_int() {
  local value="$1"
  [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]] && [[ "$value" -gt 0 ]]
}

is_nonneg_int() {
  local value="$1"
  [[ "$value" =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]
}

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
  case "$1" in
    --shard-count)
      SHARD_COUNT="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --interval-seconds)
      INTERVAL_SECONDS="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --rate-window-seconds)
      RATE_WINDOW_SECONDS="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --log-file)
      LOG_FILE="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --iterations)
      ITERATIONS="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --progress-mode)
      PROGRESS_MODE="${2:-}"
      shift 2
      ;;
    --skip-initial)
      SKIP_INITIAL=1
      shift
      ;;
    -h|--help)
      usage
      exit 0
      ;;
    *)
      echo "ERROR: unknown argument: $1" >&2
      usage >&2
      exit 2
      ;;
  esac
done

if ! is_pos_int "$SHARD_COUNT"; then
  echo "ERROR: --shard-count must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi

if ! is_pos_int "$INTERVAL_SECONDS"; then
  echo "ERROR: --interval-seconds must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi

if ! is_pos_int "$RATE_WINDOW_SECONDS"; then
  echo "ERROR: --rate-window-seconds must be a positive integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi

if ! is_nonneg_int "$ITERATIONS"; then
  echo "ERROR: --iterations must be a non-negative integer." >&2
  exit 2
fi
case "$PROGRESS_MODE" in
  light|global)
    ;;
  *)
    echo "ERROR: --progress-mode must be one of: light, global." >&2
    exit 2
    ;;
esac

SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
mkdir -p "$(dirname "$LOG_FILE")"

show_state_progress() {
  local state_files=()
  shopt -s nullglob
  state_files=(/tmp/bb_analysis_progress_state_shard_"$SHARD_COUNT"_*.env)
  shopt -u nullglob
  if [[ "${#state_files[@]}" -eq 0 ]]; then
    echo "No state files found for shard_count=$SHARD_COUNT."
    return
  fi

  local summary
  summary="$(
    awk -F= '
      FNR == 1 {
        if (file_idx++ > 0) {
          printf ","
        }
        shard_id = FILENAME
        sub(/^.*_/, "", shard_id)
        sub(/\.env$/, "", shard_id)
      }
      $1 == "ORTH_CURSOR" {
        orth = $2 + 0
        orth_sum += orth
        if (orth < orth_min || orth_count == 0) orth_min = orth
        if (orth > orth_max || orth_count == 0) orth_max = orth
        orth_count += 1
        printf "%s:%d", shard_id, orth
      }
      END {
        printf "\north_cursor_sum=%d orth_cursor_min=%d orth_cursor_max=%d shard_files=%d\n", orth_sum, orth_min, orth_max, orth_count
      }
    ' "${state_files[@]}"
  )"

  echo "State cursors:"
  printf '%s\n' "$summary"
}

snapshot() {
  {
    echo
    echo "===== $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S') ====="
    show_state_progress
    "$SCRIPT_DIR/check_analysis_rate.sh" \
      --seconds "$RATE_WINDOW_SECONDS" \
      --shard-count "$SHARD_COUNT"
    if [[ "$PROGRESS_MODE" == "global" ]]; then
      "$SCRIPT_DIR/run_analysis_backfill.sh" \
        --phase progress \
        --shard-count "$SHARD_COUNT" \
        --shard-index 0
    fi
  } >>"$LOG_FILE" 2>&1
}

iteration=0
if [[ "$SKIP_INITIAL" -eq 1 ]]; then
  sleep "$INTERVAL_SECONDS"
fi

while true; do
  snapshot
  iteration=$((iteration + 1))
  if [[ "$ITERATIONS" -gt 0 && "$iteration" -ge "$ITERATIONS" ]]; then
    break
  fi
  sleep "$INTERVAL_SECONDS"
done

  fi
fi

exec "$PYTHON_BIN" -m csvdb.orth_stream "$@"

def _repo_root() -> Path:

@dataclass(frozen=True)
class OrthStreamConfig:
    project_id: str
    dataset: str
    location: str
    bucket: str
    export_prefix: str
    local_root: Path
    rust_eval_library: str
    rust_batch_size: int
    parquet_batch_size: int
    orthogonal_version: str
    odd_weight_version: str
    min_free_disk_bytes: int
    disk_retry_seconds: float
    status_batch_size: int = 8

    @property
    def run_table_id(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.project_id}.{self.dataset}._orth_stream_runs"

    @property
    def chunk_table_id(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.project_id}.{self.dataset}._orth_stream_chunks"

    @property
    def orthogonal_true_table_id(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.project_id}.{self.dataset}.orthogonal_true_results"

    @property
    def odd_weight_true_table_id(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.project_id}.{self.dataset}.odd_weight_true_results"

    @property
    def source_table_id(self) -> str:
        return f"{self.project_id}.{self.dataset}.codes_base"

    @classmethod
    def from_env(cls) -> "OrthStreamConfig":
        base = CsvDbConfig.from_env()
        export_prefix = os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_EXPORT_PREFIX", "csvdb/orth-stream")
        local_root = Path(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_LOCAL_ROOT", "/tmp/csvdb-orth-stream"))
        rust_eval_library = os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_RUST_EVAL_LIBRARY", default_rust_eval_library_path())
        rust_batch_size = int(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_RUST_BATCH_SIZE", "10000"))
        parquet_batch_size = int(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_PARQUET_BATCH_SIZE", "50000"))
        orthogonal_version = os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_ORTHOGONAL_VERSION", "vm-rust-all-v2")
        odd_weight_version = os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_ODD_WEIGHT_VERSION", orthogonal_version)
        min_free_disk_gb = float(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_MIN_FREE_DISK_GB", "20"))
        disk_retry_seconds = float(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_DISK_RETRY_SECONDS", "30"))
        status_batch_size = int(os.environ.get("CSVDB_ANALYSIS_STATUS_BATCH_SIZE", "8"))
        return cls(
            project_id=base.project_id,
            dataset=base.dataset,
            location=base.location,
            bucket=base.bucket,
            export_prefix=export_prefix.strip("/"),
            local_root=local_root,
            rust_eval_library=rust_eval_library,
            rust_batch_size=rust_batch_size,
            parquet_batch_size=parquet_batch_size,
            orthogonal_version=orthogonal_version,
            odd_weight_version=odd_weight_version,
            min_free_disk_bytes=int(min_free_disk_gb * (1024**3)),
            disk_retry_seconds=disk_retry_seconds,
            status_batch_size=status_batch_size,
        )

    def run_root(self, run_id: str) -> Path:
        return self.local_root / run_id

    def results_dir(self, run_id: str) -> Path:
        return self.run_root(run_id) / "results"

    def orthogonal_results_dir(self, run_id: str) -> Path:
        return self.results_dir(run_id) / "orthogonal_true"

    def odd_weight_results_dir(self, run_id: str) -> Path:
        return self.results_dir(run_id) / "odd_weight_true"

    def downloads_dir(self, run_id: str) -> Path:
        return self.run_root(run_id) / "downloads"

    def disk_reservations_path(self) -> Path:
        return self.local_root / ".download_reservations.json"

    def disk_reservations_lock_path(self) -> Path:
        return self.local_root / ".download_reservations.lock"

    def export_uri(self, run_id: str) -> str:
        return f"gs://{self.bucket}/{self.export_prefix}/{run_id}/snapshot/*.parquet"

    def export_shard_uri(self, run_id: str, shard_index: int) -> str:
        return f"gs://{self.bucket}/{self.export_prefix}/{run_id}/snapshot/shard-{shard_index:05d}-*.parquet"

    def export_prefix_for_listing(self, run_id: str) -> str:
        return f"{self.export_prefix}/{run_id}/snapshot/"

class SparseTrueResultWriter:
    def __init__(
        self,
        path: Path,
        *,
        run_id: str,
        result_field: str,
        version_field: str,
        version: str,
        mode: str = "w",
    ) -> None:
        self.path = path
        self.path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
        self._file = self.path.open(mode, encoding="utf-8")
        self.run_id = run_id
        self.result_field = result_field
        self.version_field = version_field
        self.version = version

    def write_true(self, *, row_id: str, decision_source: str) -> None:
        payload = {
            "run_id": self.run_id,
            "row_id": row_id,
            self.result_field: True,
            self.version_field: self.version,
            "decision_source": decision_source,
            "created_at": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),
        }
        self._file.write(json.dumps(payload, separators=(",", ":"), sort_keys=True))
        self._file.write("\n")

    def close(self) -> None:
        self._file.close()


def _configure_logging(level_name: str) -> None:
    logging.basicConfig(
        level=getattr(logging, level_name.upper(), logging.INFO),
        format="%(asctime)s %(levelname)s %(name)s: %(message)s",
    )

    service_account_path = os.environ.get("CSVDB_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_FILE") or os.environ.get(
        "GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS"
    )
    if not service_account_path:
        default_service_account_path = Path("/etc/csvdb/service-account.json")
        if default_service_account_path.exists():
            service_account_path = str(default_service_account_path)
    if not service_account_path:
        return None

    from google.oauth2 import service_account

    return service_account.Credentials.from_service_account_file(
        service_account_path,
        scopes=["https://www.googleapis.com/auth/cloud-platform"],
    )
